meta.sprintTitle: Admin Console v1 - Super Admin Dashboard
meta.goal: Create a high-signal Super Admin dashboard page that gives a fast operational snapshot of Memorioso across growth, engagement, book creation, exports, funnel performance, and platform health so issues can be identified early without opening multiple admin modules.

basedOnPriorSprints: Sprint 40 - Early Alpha / sandbox upgrade messaging
Sprint 42 - SEO and public page readiness
Sprint 43 - Featurebase widget + messenger widget planning
Sprint 44 - Error handling and graceful user-facing messages
GA4 lead capture, funnel tracking, and conversion analysis planning
whyNow: Super admin needs a single operational homepage instead of jumping across users, exports, billing, and error tools
Memorioso is in early alpha and needs close monitoring of usage, drop-off, export reliability, and errors
This dashboard will become the control tower for launch readiness and daily operational review
dashboardIntent: Show business health at a glance
Show product engagement at a glance
Show funnel drop-off at a glance
Show system/export health at a glance
Make it easy to drill into users, projects, exports, billing, and errors

nonGoals: Deep analytics exploration with custom chart builders
Editing operational data from dashboard widgets
dependencies: Sprint 45 admin auth and RBAC foundation
Sprint 46 users and project lookup data
Sprint 47 export job and export success/failure data
Sprint 48 billing/plan state data where relevant
GA4 or internal product analytics event tracking
Application error tracking source such as app logs/Sentry-equivalent

operational: Super admin can understand overall platform state within 30 seconds of opening the dashboard
Super admin can identify whether current issues are growth-related, funnel-related, export-related, or system-health-related without opening other modules
Dashboard widgets link directly to relevant admin modules for investigation
quality: Widgets load successfully with graceful fallbacks if some data sources are unavailable
Definitions for each metric are documented and consistent across backend and UI
Dashboard is useful on day one of alpha and scales to broader launch monitoring
security: Dashboard routes and APIs are permission-guarded server-side
Only authorised roles can view sensitive operational metrics
No sensitive user-level data is exposed unnecessarily in summary widgets

personas.name: Super Admin
personas.description: Primary user of this dashboard. Needs platform-wide operational visibility and quick drill-down links.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: May see a reduced version of selected widgets if permitted, but this sprint is primarily designed for super_admin.

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: Should not see full platform-wide operational dashboard unless explicitly allowed later.

inScope: Widget-level drill-down links into existing admin modules
Date range selector for dashboard summaries
Metric definitions and backend aggregation APIs

rules: dashboard.view is required for dashboard route and API access
Widget drill-down links should only appear when user also has permission for destination module
If a role can view dashboard but not some sensitive widget, the widget must be hidden or replaced with a permission-safe state

layoutSections.purpose: Fast health snapshot for overall growth and product usage
Track landing-to-value journey and identify drop-off
Understand creation volume and export success
See platform health and recent operational issues

widgetCatalog.title: Total Users
secondaryMetrics: new users in selected date range
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Shows platform growth and whether acquisition is happening at all.

widgetCatalog.title: Active Users
secondaryMetrics: activity rate = active users / total users

widgetCatalog.title: Books Created
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Measures core product usage, not just account creation.

widgetCatalog.title: Export Success Rate
widgetCatalog.purpose: Show reliability of the highest-value output moment
widgetCatalog.primaryMetric: successful export jobs / completed export attempts
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Critical for user trust and monetisation readiness.

widgetCatalog.title: Conversion Funnel Snapshot
widgetCatalog.purpose: Show drop-off between main journey stages
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Shows where growth is leaking and where UX or technical issues may exist.

widgetCatalog.title: Recent Errors
metrics: count of recent errors in selected range
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Lets super admin quickly spot instability, especially after releases.

widgetCatalog.title: Book Status Breakdown
widgetCatalog.purpose: Show how books are distributed across lifecycle states
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Shows whether users are progressing or getting stuck.

widgetCatalog.title: Export Jobs Breakdown
widgetCatalog.purpose: Show current export workload and outcomes
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Operational visibility into export pipeline state.

widgetCatalog.title: Billing Plan Snapshot
widgetCatalog.purpose: Show current user distribution across plans where relevant
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Useful for alpha monetisation readiness and support load forecasting.

widgetCatalog.title: Recent Signups
widgetCatalog.purpose: Show newest users for quick support and acquisition awareness
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Helps during alpha when every new user matters.

widgetCatalog.title: Recent Failed Exports
widgetCatalog.purpose: Surface latest failed export jobs that may need attention
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Fast access to the most urgent value-delivery issues.

widgetCatalog.title: Recent Alerts / Operational Signals
widgetCatalog.whyUseful: Gives super admin a concise action list rather than only passive stats.

superAdminUsefulInsights.insight: Are users coming in but not creating books?
Are users creating books but failing at export?
Is the product stable after release?
Are monetisation and sandbox states aligned?

totalUsers.definition: All distinct registered user accounts excluding deleted/test accounts unless explicitly included by filter.

activeUsers.definition: Distinct users with qualifying activity in selected period, such as login, book creation, editing, answering prompts, or export attempt.

booksCreated.definition: Count of distinct book/project records created in selected period and all-time total where applicable.

exportSuccessRate.definition: Succeeded export jobs divided by succeeded + failed export jobs in selected period. Exclude queued/processing from denominator.

conversionFunnel.definition: A stage-based summary of key user journey events from landing to PDF generation, using tracked analytics/internal events.

recentErrors.definition: Recent application or system errors captured in selected period, grouped by severity/category, excluding noise if possible.

fallbackStrategy: If some analytics source is unavailable, show widget with unavailable state rather than breaking the page
Allow dashboard to render partial data source success
Include last updated timestamp per widget or per dashboard response

userStories.title: See platform health quickly
userStories.iWant: to see a concise operational summary on admin home
userStories.soThat: I can assess health without opening multiple modules

userStories.title: See growth and engagement separately
userStories.iWant: to compare total users, active users, and books created
userStories.soThat: I can tell whether users are signing up and actually using the product

userStories.title: See funnel drop-off
userStories.iWant: to view a snapshot of the main conversion journey
userStories.soThat: I can identify the highest-friction step

userStories.title: See export reliability
userStories.iWant: to monitor export success and recent failures
userStories.soThat: I can respond quickly to user-impacting issues

userStories.title: See recent errors
userStories.iWant: to monitor system issues and affected areas

functionalRequirements.title: Dashboard home route
requirements: Provide /admin dashboard homepage as the primary landing page after admin login
Dashboard must be accessible only to roles with dashboard.view
Dashboard must load summary widgets from server-side aggregated APIs

functionalRequirements.title: Date range support
requirements: Allow selected ranges such as today, 7 days, 30 days, and custom range
All range-sensitive widgets should update together
Widgets using all-time totals may still show range-based secondary change values

functionalRequirements.title: Top KPI cards
requirements: Display total users
Display active users
Display books created
Display export success rate
Each KPI card should support primary value, secondary comparison, and drill-down link where allowed

functionalRequirements.title: Conversion funnel snapshot
requirements: Show main funnel stages from landing to PDF generation
Show counts and percentages between stages
Highlight largest drop-off stage
Allow drill-down to deeper analytics page later

functionalRequirements.title: Recent errors widget
requirements: Show recent error count in selected range
Show top categories and affected users count where available
Show latest/severe recent errors in compact list or summary panel
Link to detailed errors page when implemented

functionalRequirements.title: Books and export operational widgets
requirements: Show book status breakdown
Show export jobs breakdown
Show recent failed exports
Allow drill-down to projects/exports admin pages

functionalRequirements.title: Recent operational activity widgets
requirements: Show recent signups
Show recent failed exports
Show recent alerts or operational signals if available
Keep lists short and scannable

functionalRequirements.title: Billing/plan snapshot
requirements: Show plan distribution if billing data exists
At minimum show free/trial/premium/manual entitlement counts
Hide widget gracefully if billing module unavailable

functionalRequirements.title: Graceful loading and partial failure handling
requirements: Dashboard must still render if one or more widgets fail to load
Each widget should support loading, empty, unavailable, and error states
Do not block the whole dashboard on a single slow data source

functionalRequirements.title: Permission-aware dashboard
requirements: Only show widgets the current role is allowed to view
Only show drill-down actions if destination permission exists
Sensitive widgets should not leak hidden data through summary text

apiSurface.note: You can use one aggregated dashboard endpoint or multiple widget endpoints. Prefer one dashboard-summary endpoint for initial load plus optional widget-specific endpoints later.

recommendedEndpoints.purpose: Return all top-level dashboard widget data for selected range
Return funnel stage counts and conversion percentages
Return recent error summary widget data

pages.title: Dashboard
pages.purpose: Operational home for super admin with platform-wide widgets and drill-down links.

uxRules: Use scan-friendly layout with strong visual hierarchy
Use charts only where they improve clarity, not decoration
Every widget should have a title, metric definition tooltip, and drill-down action where relevant
Keep the page useful even with low-volume alpha traffic

kpiCards: Show previous-period delta in smaller text
Use a subtle trend indicator if available
funnelWidget: Use step cards or vertical funnel bars
Show count and conversion rate between each step
Highlight biggest drop in the journey
statusBreakdowns: Use donut or horizontal bar chart

technicalTasks.title: Define dashboard metrics contract
technicalTasks.description: Document exact metric definitions, date-range behaviour, exclusions, and comparison rules.

technicalTasks.title: Build dashboard summary aggregation service
technicalTasks.description: Create backend aggregation layer that composes users, projects, exports, billing, analytics, and error summaries into one dashboard response.

technicalTasks.title: Build dashboard APIs
technicalTasks.description: Implement permission-guarded admin dashboard endpoints for summary and optional widget drill-down data.

technicalTasks.title: Build dashboard UI shell
technicalTasks.description: Create /admin dashboard page, section layout, range selector, and widget grid.

technicalTasks.title: Implement KPI widgets
technicalTasks.description: Build KPI cards for total users, active users, books created, and export success rate.

technicalTasks.title: Implement funnel widget
technicalTasks.description: Build conversion funnel snapshot widget using tracked stage events.

technicalTasks.title: Implement reliability widgets
technicalTasks.description: Build recent errors summary, export breakdown, and recent failed exports widgets.

technicalTasks.title: Implement growth/activity widgets
technicalTasks.description: Build recent signups, book status breakdown, and optional billing plan snapshot widgets.

technicalTasks.title: Permission-aware widget rendering
technicalTasks.description: Ensure widgets and drill-down links render only when current admin has required permissions.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add unit, integration, and E2E coverage for dashboard metrics and widget rendering.

metricComputationNotes.rangeComparison: Compare selected date range to immediately preceding equal-length period where possible.
metricComputationNotes.booksCreatedQualifyingRule: Count books by created_at in selected range, regardless of current state.
metricComputationNotes.exportSuccessRule: Use export job results from selected range; exclude queued and processing from denominator.
metricComputationNotes.recentErrorsRule: Prefer deduplicated grouped errors plus affected users rather than raw event volume only.

loggingNotes: Log dashboard failures by widget/source to help operational debugging
Do not expose raw secrets or PII in logs
Track which data source was unavailable when partial rendering occurs

securityRequirements: Dashboard endpoints must be protected by admin auth and dashboard.view permission
Only show summary-level data by default; detailed user-level data belongs in drill-down pages
Sensitive widgets such as billing snapshot must follow permission checks
Dashboard must remain noindex and excluded from public sitemap
Unauthorised roles must not infer hidden widget data via partial payload leaks

acceptanceCriteria.then: they see KPI widgets for total users, active users, books created, and export success rate
they see a conversion funnel snapshot with stage counts, conversion percentages, and largest drop-off
they see recent errors summary and recent failed exports

acceptanceCriteria.given: one widget data source is unavailable
acceptanceCriteria.then: the rest of the dashboard still renders and the failing widget shows an unavailable state

acceptanceCriteria.given: an admin role without permission for a drill-down module
acceptanceCriteria.then: that widget link/action is hidden or disabled safely

acceptanceCriteria.when: they attempt to access dashboard route or API

unitTests: metric definition helpers compute totals and deltas correctly
export success rate denominator excludes queued and processing jobs
funnel computation identifies largest drop-off correctly
widget visibility logic respects role permissions
integrationTests: dashboard summary aggregates user/project/export data correctly
partial data source failure still returns renderable response
billing widget omitted or hidden for roles without billing permission
e2eTests: super_admin opens dashboard and sees all primary widgets
drill-down click opens allowed destination module
support_admin sees restricted dashboard version if configured
negativeTests: unauthorised actor cannot access dashboard API
hidden widget data is not leaked in payload for disallowed roles
invalid date range input returns safe validation error

risks.impact: Loss of trust in admin dashboard
risks.mitigation: Define and document each metric centrally and reuse shared aggregation logic

risks.risk: Dashboard becomes noisy and hard to scan
risks.mitigation: Keep v1 focused on a small set of high-signal widgets and clear hierarchy

risks.risk: Some analytics sources are missing in early alpha
risks.mitigation: Support graceful unavailable states and ship with core internal metrics first
Use pre-aggregations/caching where needed and separate heavy widgets if necessary

recommendedOrder: Define metric contract and range behaviour
Build dashboard page shell and range selector
Harden permission logic and partial failure handling
futureSprintHooks: Sprint 50: Feature flags and operational controls

definitionOfDone: Admin dashboard homepage exists with key operational widgets
Top KPI cards show total users, active users, books created, and export success rate
Conversion funnel snapshot exists and is range-aware
Recent failed exports and recent signups widgets exist
Widget drill-down links connect to relevant admin modules
Dashboard renders gracefully under partial data source failures
Permissions are enforced server-side and in widget rendering
Automated tests cover metric logic, permissions, and core dashboard rendering
